messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following pension fund member privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"PensionSafe processes your name, national insurance number, date of birth, salary details, and bank account information to administer your pension scheme. Processing is necessary for the performance of the pension contract and to comply with pension legislation. Beneficiary nominations are held on file and updated at your request. Pension records are retained for the duration of membership and for 12 years after scheme closure. You have the right to access, correct, port, restrict, and object to your data. Contact dataprotection@pensionsafe.co.uk. Our DPO is at dpo@pensionsafe.co.uk. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach. We use Mercer as our pension administration platform under a signed DPA with EEA-only data processing."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) both stated and appropriate for pension administration.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Beneficiary nominations may reveal family structure and dependants with health conditions. Salary data tied to disability or ill-health retirement can also be special category. No safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Pension administration, beneficiary management, and regulatory compliance are clearly stated.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for pension scheme administration.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Duration of membership plus 12 years post-scheme closure is stated and appropriate for pension regulatory requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) listed. Erasure (Art. 17) absent — likely limited by legal retention obligations, but this should be explained.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Mercer named as administration platform provider under a signed DPA with EEA-only processing.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EEA-only data processing confirmed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@pensionsafe.co.uk disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (beneficiary and ill-health data sensitivity not addressed), Art. 15-21 (erasure right absent without explanation)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that beneficiary nominations and ill-health records may contain special category data and state appropriate safeguards
- Add the erasure right with an explanation that it is limited by pension legal retention obligations under Art. 17(3)(b)
